analysis_result: 3/8 (37.5%) of MPNST-G1 tumors show arm-level 18q loss. Individual 18q log2 ratios for the 8 MPNST-G1 samples (tumor vs benign-NF reference panel): M2252 = -0.401 (loss), M2346 = -0.311 (loss), M2372 = -0.177 (loss), M1014 = -0.056, M803 = -0.024, M3048 = +0.019, M6712 = +0.039, M3398 = +0.102. Using the CONUMEE-standard threshold of log2 ratio < -0.15 for arm-level loss. At a stricter threshold of -0.20, 2/8 (25%) qualify; at a looser threshold of -0.10, still 3/8 (37.5%). The reference panel (benign/cutaneous NF, n=63) shows 0/63 arm-level 18q losses at every threshold tested (specificity check).
denominator_used: N = 8 MPNST-G1 samples (Methylation_Group == 'MPNST_G1' in Supplementary Data 1: M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048, M3398, M6712, M803). All 8 had available IDAT pairs.
assumptions_made: CONUMEE R package was not installed; re-implemented the core CONUMEE logic in Python using methylprep for raw IDAT parsing. Total probe intensity computed as minfi-style Meth+Unmeth signal: Type II probes = Grn[AddrA] + Red[AddrA]; Type I Grn = Grn[AddrA] + Grn[AddrB]; Type I Red = Red[AddrA] + Red[AddrB]. Probes restricted to autosomes (chr1-22) with non-missing CHR/MAPINFO coordinates (hg19). Invalid probes (any NaN or zero intensity across 108 samples) were dropped, leaving 844,537 probes. Per-sample median log2 centering was applied to remove global intensity differences. Reference panel was the 63 non-MPNST, non-premalignant neurofibroma samples (Benign_NF-G4 n=20, Cutaneous NF_G6/G7 n=33, Sporadic Spinal NF_G5 n=10); per-probe reference = median log2 intensity across these 63 samples. Per-sample per-probe log2 ratio = sample_centered - reference_median. Chromosome arms were defined using hg19 UCSC cytoBand centromere coordinates: probes with MAPINFO < centromere_start assigned to p arm, MAPINFO > centromere_end assigned to q arm, probes in the centromeric gap (n=211 on chr18) were excluded. For chr18: 3,711 probes on 18p and 10,956 probes on 18q. Arm-level call = median of per-probe log2 ratios within the arm for each sample. Loss threshold = log2 ratio < -0.15 (CONUMEE default for arm-level heterozygous loss in tumor samples with modest purity). The reference panel itself shows tight arm-level log2 distribution (mean ~0, median 0, std 0.052; 99th pct = 0.170), indicating the -0.15 threshold is well outside diploid noise. Chromosome-X probes excluded (sex-biased).
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx Clinical Data sheet: 108 samples with Methylation_Group assignments. 8 are MPNST_G1.
   - GSE207207_idats/: 108 paired Grn/Red IDAT files, MethylationEPIC manifest (GPL21145, hg19).
   - Manifest: 848,166 autosomal probes with valid coordinates after filtering controls and missing CHR/MAPINFO.

B. COHORT
   - Filter: Methylation_Group == 'MPNST_G1' -> 8 samples (M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048, M3398, M6712, M803)
   - All 8 have IDAT pairs available -> final analytic N = 8
   - Reference panel: benign/cutaneous neurofibromas (Benign_NF-G4 + Cutaneous NF_G6 + Cutaneous NF_G7 + Sporadic Spinal NF_G5) = 63 samples

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Per-probe total intensity (minfi convention): Type II = Grn[AddrA]+Red[AddrA]; Type I Grn = Grn[AddrA]+Grn[AddrB]; Type I Red = Red[AddrA]+Red[AddrB]
   - Probes dropped if any NaN or zero across 108 samples -> 844,537 valid probes retained
   - 18p probes: n=3,711 (MAPINFO < 15,460,898 on chr18)
   - 18q probes: n=10,956 (MAPINFO > 19,460,898 on chr18)
   - Centromeric probes excluded: 211

D. COMPUTATION
   1. Parsed 108 IDAT pairs with methylprep.IdatDataset -> intensity matrix (844,537 probes x 108 samples)
   2. Log2-transformed: log2(intensity + 1)
   3. Per-sample median centering: log_centered[p,s] = log_int[p,s] - median_s(log_int[.,s])
   4. Per-probe reference = median(log_centered[p, reference_samples]) over 63 reference samples
   5. Per-sample per-probe log2 ratio = log_centered[p,s] - reference[p]
   6. Arm-level call per sample = median over probes within the arm
   7. 18q loss call: log2 ratio < -0.15

   18q log2 ratios for MPNST-G1 samples:
     M1014: -0.056    (no loss)
     M2252: -0.401    (LOSS)
     M2346: -0.311    (LOSS)
     M2372: -0.177    (LOSS)
     M3048: +0.019    (no loss)
     M3398: +0.102    (no loss)
     M6712: +0.039    (no loss)
     M803:  -0.024    (no loss)

   Contrast - 18q log2 ratios in MPNST-G2 (n=8):  all values -0.139 to +0.033, zero losses at threshold -0.15
   Contrast - 18q log2 ratios in Benign_NF-G4 (n=20): all values -0.040 to +0.063, zero losses

   Sensitivity to threshold:
     log2 < -0.05: 4/8 (50%)
     log2 < -0.10: 3/8 (37.5%)
     log2 < -0.15: 3/8 (37.5%)  <- chosen CONUMEE-standard threshold
     log2 < -0.20: 2/8 (25.0%)
     log2 < -0.30: 2/8 (25.0%)

   Specificity check (18q loss rate in benign/cutaneous NF reference at same thresholds): 0/63 at every threshold.

E. RESULT
   3/8 (37.5%) of MPNST-G1 tumors show 18q loss by arm-level log2 ratio analysis.

   The three samples with 18q loss (M2252, M2346, M2372) each also show concomitant 18p loss (values -0.302, -0.306, -0.161), consistent with whole-chromosome 18 loss in these cases.

F. CAVEATS
   - CONUMEE R package was not installed, so its CBS segmentation step was not available. Arm-level median log2 ratio is a reasonable approximation of CONUMEE's arm-level call when a whole arm is involved (which appears to be the case for the three 18q-loss MPNST-G1 samples), but focal segmental events within 18q that do not span most of the arm would be diluted in an arm-median summary.
   - Threshold choice affects the point estimate: 25-50% depending on threshold. The middle of that range (37.5% at -0.10 to -0.15) is most consistent with CONUMEE defaults and with the specificity of the reference panel.
   - Reference panel is not a panel of tumor-free reference samples (the paper has no true normal tissue). Benign/cutaneous neurofibromas are the most diploid tissues in the dataset and are a reasonable proxy, but any systematic CNA present across the NF reference would be absorbed into the baseline. Chr18 is not a recurrent CNA site in benign NF, so this should not bias 18q calls.
   - Small n (8) gives a wide 95% CI on the proportion (~9% to 76% binomial exact CI). The 37.5% point estimate should be interpreted in that context.
